tools.name: ppds_auth_who
tools.description: Get the current authentication profile context including identity, connected environment, and token status. Use this to understand which Dataverse environment queries will run against.

tools.name: ppds_connection_references_analyze
tools.description: Analyze connection references for orphaned references (not used by any flow) and orphaned flows (referencing missing connection references). Useful for deployment cleanup.

tools.name: ppds_connection_references_get
tools.description: Get full details of a specific connection reference including dependent flows and connection info. Use the logicalName from ppds_connection_references_list.

tools.name: ppds_connection_references_list
tools.description: List connection references in the current environment. Shows connector bindings and whether connections are bound. Optionally filter by solution name.

tools.name: ppds_custom_apis_list
tools.description: List all Custom APIs registered in the Dataverse environment. Custom APIs are developer-defined messages that extend the Dataverse API surface with custom business logic. Returns each API with its request parameters and response properties.

tools.name: ppds_data_analyze
tools.description: Analyze data for a Dataverse entity. Returns record count, primary attributes, and sample records. Use this to understand the data in an entity before querying.

tools.name: ppds_data_providers_list
tools.description: List all virtual entity data sources and data providers registered in the Dataverse environment. Data providers implement plugin-based virtual entity adapters that connect Dataverse virtual entities to external data sources. Returns both data source definitions and their associated data provider plugin bindings.

tools.name: ppds_data_schema
tools.description: Get the schema (fields/attributes) for a Dataverse entity. Returns attribute names, types, and metadata. Use this to understand entity structure before querying.

tools.name: ppds_env_list
tools.description: List available Dataverse environments. Use this to discover which environments are accessible with the current authentication profile. The currently selected environment is marked with isActive=true.

tools.name: ppds_env_select
tools.description: Select a Dataverse environment for subsequent queries. You can specify the environment by URL (e.g., 'https://contoso.crm.dynamics.com'), display name (e.g., 'Contoso Production'), or unique name. All subsequent ppds tools will use this environment.

tools.name: ppds_environment_variables_get
tools.description: Get full details of a specific environment variable including description, type, and values. Use the schemaName from ppds_environment_variables_list.

tools.name: ppds_environment_variables_list
tools.description: List environment variable definitions and their current values. Shows default vs current values, type, and override status. Optionally filter by solution name.

tools.name: ppds_environment_variables_set
tools.description: Set the current value of an environment variable. Creates the value record if none exists. AI agents can use this to fix misconfigurations during deployment troubleshooting.

tools.name: ppds_import_jobs_get
tools.description: Get full details of a specific import job including the XML import log. Use the id from ppds_import_jobs_list. The import log XML contains detailed component-level success/failure information for troubleshooting failed imports.

tools.name: ppds_import_jobs_list
tools.description: List recent solution import jobs for the current environment. Shows import status, progress, solution name, and timing. Use this to check if a solution import succeeded or failed.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_add_column
tools.description: Adds a new column (attribute) to an existing Dataverse table. Supports all standard column types. Use dry-run to validate before committing.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_add_option_value
tools.description: Adds a new option value to an existing option set (global or local choice). Returns the assigned integer value.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_create_choice
tools.description: Creates a new global choice (option set) in the specified solution. Options are provided as a JSON array of {label, value} objects. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_create_key
tools.description: Creates an alternate key on a Dataverse table. Attributes can be provided as a comma-separated list or a JSON array of column logical names. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_create_relationship
tools.description: Creates a relationship (one-to-many or many-to-many) between two Dataverse tables. For one-to-many, a lookup column is created on the referencing entity. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_create_table
tools.description: Creates a new Dataverse table (entity) in the specified solution. Supports dry-run mode for validation without persisting changes. Use ppds_metadata_entities to verify the table was created.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_entities
tools.description: Lists all entities (tables) in the connected Dataverse environment. Returns entity logical names, display names, schema names, and ownership type. Use this to discover available entities before querying with ppds_metadata_entity for full details.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_entity
tools.description: Get detailed metadata for a Dataverse entity including attributes, relationships, and keys. Use this to understand entity structure, field types, and how entities relate to each other.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_update_choice
tools.description: Updates an existing global choice (option set). Only the provided optional fields are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_update_column
tools.description: Updates an existing column (attribute) on a Dataverse table. Only the provided optional fields are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_update_relationship
tools.description: Updates an existing Dataverse relationship's cascade configuration. Only the provided optional fields are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_metadata_update_table
tools.description: Updates an existing Dataverse table (entity). Only the provided optional fields are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged. Supports dry-run mode.

tools.name: ppds_plugin_traces_delete
tools.description: Delete plugin trace logs. Provide exactly one mode: specific IDs for targeted deletion, olderThanDays for age-based cleanup, or filter parameters (typeName, messageName, primaryEntity, errorsOnly) for criteria-based deletion.

tools.name: ppds_plugin_traces_get
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific plugin trace including the full message block (trace output) and exception details. Use the trace ID from ppds_plugin_traces_list.

tools.name: ppds_plugin_traces_list
tools.description: List plugin trace logs from Dataverse. Use this to find plugin execution logs, identify errors, and debug plugin behavior. Requires trace logging to be enabled in the environment.

tools.name: ppds_plugin_traces_timeline
tools.description: Build an execution timeline showing all plugin executions for a single transaction. Use the correlationId from ppds_plugin_traces_list to see how plugins chain together and identify performance bottlenecks.

tools.name: ppds_plugins_get
tools.description: Get detailed information for a specific plugin registration entity (assembly, package, type, step, or image). Use ppds_plugins_list first to discover entity names/IDs.

tools.name: ppds_plugins_list
tools.description: List registered plugin assemblies in the Dataverse environment. Shows plugin types and their registered steps (message/entity/stage combinations). By default excludes hidden system assemblies and Microsoft.* assemblies.

tools.name: ppds_query_fetch
tools.description: Execute a FetchXML query against Dataverse. Use this when you have raw FetchXML or need advanced query features not available in SQL. Prefer ppds_query_sql for simpler queries.

tools.name: ppds_query_sql
tools.description: Execute a SQL SELECT query against Dataverse. The SQL is transpiled to FetchXML internally. Supports JOINs, WHERE, ORDER BY, TOP, and aggregate functions. Example: SELECT name, revenue FROM account WHERE statecode = 0 ORDER BY revenue DESC

tools.name: ppds_service_endpoints_list
tools.description: List all service endpoints and webhooks registered in the Dataverse environment. Includes Azure Service Bus (Queue, Topic, EventHub), REST endpoints, and HTTP webhooks. Used for event-driven integrations that receive Dataverse change notifications.

tools.name: ppds_solutions_components
tools.description: Get components of a Dataverse solution. Returns entities, workflows, plugins, and other components. Use the solution id from ppds_solutions_list. Optionally filter by component type code.

tools.name: ppds_solutions_list
tools.description: List Dataverse solutions for the current environment. Shows solution name, version, publisher, and managed status. Use includeManaged to also show managed (system) solutions.

tools.name: ppds_web_resources_get
tools.description: Get web resource content for viewing or analysis. Returns decoded text content for text types (JS, HTML, CSS, XML, etc.) and metadata-only for binary types (PNG, JPG, GIF). Use the id from ppds_web_resources_list.

tools.name: ppds_web_resources_list
tools.description: List web resources in a Dataverse environment, optionally filtered by solution. Returns name, type, managed status, and modification info. Use textOnly=true (default) to exclude binary types like images. Supports pagination via maxRows and nextPageToken parameters — when more records exist the response includes a nextPageToken to pass on the next call.

tools.name: ppds_web_resources_publish
tools.description: Publish specific web resources to make changes live. After updating web resource content, you must publish for changes to take effect. Provide one or more web resource IDs.
